articles.headline: India Unveils Detailed Implementation Strategy for National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) today released the comprehensive implementation strategy for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The strategy focuses on phased manufacturing capabilities, demand creation mechanisms, and infrastructure development for green hydrogen production and utilization.
• Key components include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing, pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors, and a robust R&D framework.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are highlighted as crucial for achieving the mission's ambitious targets.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Aims to achieve 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
Key components include SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry.
Focus on reducing fossil fuel imports and creating export opportunities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in achieving the mission's objectives.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits, including job creation and industrial development.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Air Quality Action Plans, Citing Right to Life
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today issued strong directives to several state governments and Union Territories regarding the urgent implementation of their Air Quality Action Plans.
• The Court emphasized that clean air is an integral part of the fundamental 'Right to Life' under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• It expressed concern over the persistent high levels of air pollution, especially in major urban centers, despite existing policies and guidelines.
• States have been given a strict deadline to submit compliance reports on measures taken, including curbing stubble burning, industrial emissions, and vehicular pollution.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's proactive role in environmental governance and public health.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Supreme Court has interpreted Right to Life to include Right to Clean Environment.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) launched in 2019 to reduce PM2.5 and PM10 concentrations by 20-30% by 2024.
Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on environmental policy formulation and implementation in India.
Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in addressing air pollution in India, including socio-economic and technological aspects.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives (e.g., NCAP) and suggest further measures.
Examine the concept of environmental justice and the role of the judiciary in upholding it.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of a polluted city skyline with a clear foreground showing a judge's gavel.
articles.trivia: Delhi was once ranked the most polluted capital city in the world, often experiencing 'severe' air quality days during winter months.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, reflecting a cautious approach to inflation management while supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted global uncertainties and potential risks from food price volatility.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target, indicating that a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance would continue.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts would depend on sustained disinflation and robust economic data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
MPC sets the policy interest rates to achieve inflation target (currently 4% +/- 2%).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Headline inflation is measured by Consumer Price Index (CPI) in India.
RBI's primary objective is price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
Discuss the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy (e.g., banking, industry, consumers).
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Examine the role of global economic factors in shaping India's monetary policy.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the RBI logo with upward-trending inflation and downward-trending growth lines.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial hot-fire test for the semi-cryogenic engine of its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• This test marks a significant milestone in the development of NGLV, which is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
• NGLV aims to replace the existing PSLV and GSLV rockets, catering to the growing demands of India's space missions, including human spaceflight and commercial launches.
• The vehicle will utilize green propellants and modular architecture, enhancing its versatility and environmental sustainability.
• ISRO plans for NGLV to be operational by the early 2030s, positioning India strongly in the global space launch market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: NGLV aims to replace PSLV and GSLV.
It is designed as a reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
Utilizes semi-cryogenic engines and green propellants.
Expected to be operational by early 2030s.
ISRO is India's national space agency, headquartered in Bengaluru.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Next-Generation Launch Vehicles for India's space program and national interests.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements involved in designing and testing semi-cryogenic engines and reusable launch vehicles.
Evaluate the potential impact of NGLV on India's commercial space capabilities and its role in the global space market.
Examine how NGLV aligns with India's long-term goals in space exploration and human spaceflight (e.g., Gaganyaan).
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the NGLV taking off, with the ISRO logo prominently displayed.
articles.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizes Maritime Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue concluded today, with both sides reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key discussions revolved around enhancing maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, including joint patrols and information sharing.
• Economic integration was a major agenda item, with talks on strengthening supply chain resilience and accelerating trade and investment flows.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its 'Act East' policy, aiming for deeper engagement across political, security, economic, and socio-cultural pillars.
• A joint statement highlighted cooperation on climate change, digital transformation, and people-to-people exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, upgraded to a Full Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
India's 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy' (1991).
ASEAN comprises 10 member states.
Indo-Pacific region is critical for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's strategic partnership with ASEAN in the context of its 'Act East Policy' and Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for enhancing economic and security cooperation between India and ASEAN.
Evaluate the role of ASEAN centrality in maintaining regional stability and promoting multilateralism.
Examine how India-ASEAN cooperation can counter emerging geopolitical challenges in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states waving together.
articles.trivia: The ASEAN Secretariat is located in Jakarta, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the proposed Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, recommending several crucial amendments.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing user consent mechanisms, and refining the definition of 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• Concerns regarding government exemptions and data localization requirements were addressed, with calls for clearer guidelines and independent oversight.
• The committee emphasized the need for a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The bill is expected to be tabled for discussion and passage in the ongoing winter session, potentially with some of the suggested changes incorporated.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
Based on recommendations of the Srikrishna Committee report (2018).
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 (Puttaswamy judgment).
Aims to establish a Data Protection Board of India.
Distinguishes between data fiduciary (who determines purpose and means of processing) and data principal (individual whose data is processed).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill and its potential impact on individual privacy and the digital economy.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the role of a Data Protection Board in enforcing data protection regulations and addressing grievances.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's bill compares, especially concerning cross-border data flows.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of digital data flowing through a secure network with a padlock icon.
articles.trivia: The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is considered one of the strictest data privacy laws globally.

articles.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Projected at 7.2%, Driven by Services and Manufacturing Sector
articles.explainer:
• Leading economic think tanks and rating agencies today released their projections for India's Q3 (October-December) GDP growth, averaging around 7.2%.
• The robust growth is attributed to strong performance in the services sector, particularly financial and IT services, and a resilient manufacturing output.
• Festival season demand and increased government capital expenditure are cited as key drivers contributing to domestic consumption and investment.
• While global headwinds persist, India's domestic demand-led recovery continues to show resilience, positioning it as a fast-growing major economy.
• However, concerns remain regarding rural demand recovery and potential impacts of global commodity price fluctuations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific period.
India's financial year runs from April 1 to March 31.
Q3 refers to the October-December quarter.
Key drivers of GDP include private consumption, government consumption, investment, and net exports.
National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI releases official GDP data.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's projected GDP growth in Q3 and the role of different economic sectors.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining high economic growth in India amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government fiscal and monetary policies in fostering economic recovery and stability.
Examine the implications of India's growth trajectory for employment generation and poverty reduction.
articles.imageDescription: A bar graph showing India's GDP growth trend with various sectoral contributions.
articles.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2030.

articles.headline: Ministry of Urban Affairs Launches New Framework for Urban Flood Resilience Planning
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today unveiled a comprehensive framework to enhance urban flood resilience across Indian cities.
• The framework emphasizes integrated water management, nature-based solutions, and upgrading existing drainage infrastructure.
• It mandates the preparation of city-specific Urban Flood Resilience Plans, incorporating early warning systems and community participation.
• The initiative comes in response to increasing frequency and intensity of urban flooding events exacerbated by climate change and unplanned urbanization.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to states and local bodies for effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is often caused by inadequate drainage, encroachment on natural water bodies, and intense rainfall.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) provides guidelines for urban flood management.
Smart Cities Mission aims to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
Nature-based solutions include green infrastructure like rain gardens and permeable pavements.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry for urban development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the primary causes of urban flooding in India and its socio-economic consequences.
Analyze the key features of the new Urban Flood Resilience Planning framework and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and community engagement in building flood-resilient cities.
Examine how climate change exacerbates urban flooding and the importance of adaptation strategies.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city with a green infrastructure overlay, showing water flowing into designated green spaces.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a major natural drain, has been significantly narrowed and polluted, contributing to severe flooding.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone: Over 50 Crore ABHA IDs Generated
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced today that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has successfully generated over 50 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) IDs.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in creating a digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records and services.
• ABHA IDs allow individuals to link their health records, facilitating easier access for healthcare providers and reducing paperwork.
• The mission aims to standardize digital health infrastructure, promoting interoperability and data security across the healthcare continuum.
• Expanding the network of healthcare facilities and providers registered on ABDM remains a key focus for broader adoption.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched in September 2021.
Aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique health identification number.
National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Part of the broader Ayushman Bharat program, which includes PM-JAY.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key components of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and its potential to transform India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of digital health IDs and electronic health records.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital public infrastructure in achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare delivery.
Examine the concerns related to data privacy and security within the ABDM framework and proposed safeguards.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital health icons, with the ABHA logo in the center.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) today released updated guidelines for cybersecurity practices targeting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The revised framework mandates stricter compliance for organizations operating in sectors like energy, finance, telecommunications, and transport.
• Key directives include implementing multi-factor authentication, regular security audits, and establishing robust incident response protocols.
• The move aims to bolster India's defenses against sophisticated cyber threats and protect essential services from potential disruptions.
• Collaboration between government agencies and private sector entities is stressed for effective implementation and threat intelligence sharing.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) is defined under the IT Act, 2000.
NCIIPC is mandated to protect CII in India.
Cybersecurity frameworks often include ISO 27001, NIST Cybersecurity Framework.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds layers of security beyond just a password.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyberattacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key features of CERT-In's new guidelines and their potential effectiveness in enhancing cybersecurity.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing robust cybersecurity measures across diverse critical sectors in India.
Examine the importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in combating global cyber threats.
articles.imageDescription: A network of digital lines forming a shield, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: The Stuxnet computer worm, discovered in 2010, was one of the first known cyberweapons used to target industrial control systems.

articles.headline: India and African Nations Discuss Feasibility of New Trade Corridor for Enhanced Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• India and several African nations initiated discussions today on the feasibility of establishing a dedicated trade corridor to boost economic ties.
• The proposed corridor aims to streamline logistics, reduce trade costs, and facilitate greater movement of goods, services, and investments between India and Africa.
• Discussions focused on infrastructure development, digital connectivity, and harmonizing trade regulations to create a seamless economic pathway.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to strengthening South-South cooperation and leveraging Africa's growth potential.
• Experts highlight the potential for collaboration in areas like renewable energy, agriculture, and healthcare through this enhanced connectivity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India has traditionally strong ties with African nations.
India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is a major platform for cooperation.
India is a significant investor and trading partner for Africa.
Connectivity projects like INSTC aim to reduce transit times and costs.
Focus on shared priorities like sustainable development, climate action, and capacity building.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of enhancing economic connectivity between India and African nations.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges in establishing a new trade corridor, considering geopolitical and logistical factors.
Evaluate how India's engagement with Africa differs from that of other major global powers and its implications for South-South cooperation.
Examine the specific sectors where India-Africa cooperation can be further strengthened through such initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing a conceptual trade route connecting India and various African countries.
articles.trivia: Africa is home to the world's largest free trade area, the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA).

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches Enhanced National Plan for Wetlands Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched an upgraded National Plan for Wetlands Conservation and Management, focusing on integrated approaches.
• The plan emphasizes scientific inventory, ecological restoration, community participation, and sustainable utilization of wetland resources.
• It aims to protect and restore India's diverse wetlands, which play crucial roles in biodiversity, water purification, and climate change mitigation.
• Special attention will be given to Ramsar Sites and other ecologically sensitive wetlands, with enhanced monitoring and enforcement mechanisms.
• The initiative seeks to align national efforts with international conventions like the Ramsar Convention.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Ramsar Convention is an intergovernmental treaty for the conservation and wise use of wetlands.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites (e.g., Chilika Lake, Loktak Lake).
Wetlands provide ecosystem services like flood control, water purification, and habitat for wildlife.
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal agency for wetland conservation.
Wetland (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017, provide the regulatory framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the key provisions of the enhanced National Plan for Wetlands Conservation and its potential impact on biodiversity and local livelihoods.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing effective wetland conservation strategies, including encroachment and pollution.
Examine the importance of community participation and integrated management approaches in wetland protection.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: The world's largest wetland is the Pantanal, primarily located in Brazil, Bolivia, and Paraguay.

articles.headline: Parliament Debates Centre-State Fiscal Devolution Amidst GST Compensation Cess Discussions
articles.explainer:
• The winter session of Parliament witnessed a heated debate today on the issues of Centre-State fiscal devolution and the future of GST compensation cess.
• Several states raised concerns over reduced fiscal space, demanding a clear roadmap for revenue sharing beyond the GST compensation period.
• The Union Finance Minister highlighted the recommendations of the latest Finance Commission and measures taken to support states' finances.
• Opposition parties called for greater transparency and autonomy for states in managing their fiscal resources.
• The discussions underscore the ongoing complexities of fiscal federalism in India and the need for cooperative solutions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Finance Commission is a constitutional body (Article 280) that recommends distribution of taxes between Centre and States.
GST Compensation Cess was introduced for 5 years (until June 2022) to compensate states for revenue loss due to GST implementation.
15th Finance Commission recommended a 41% share for states in the divisible pool of central taxes.
Fiscal federalism refers to the division of financial powers and responsibilities between different levels of government.
Union List, State List, and Concurrent List define legislative powers, impacting revenue generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by states in managing their finances in the post-GST regime, particularly concerning fiscal devolution.
Analyze the role of the Finance Commission in promoting cooperative fiscal federalism in India.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the continuation of GST compensation cess and alternative mechanisms for supporting state revenues.
Examine the implications of Centre-State fiscal relations for overall economic stability and development in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing two hands, one representing the Centre and the other states, exchanging money.
articles.trivia: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951.

articles.headline: Government Initiates Public Consultation on National AI Ethics Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a public consultation process for India's proposed National AI Ethics Framework.
• The framework aims to guide the ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across various sectors, ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability.
• Key principles include human-centric AI, data privacy, bias mitigation, and responsible innovation.
• The consultation seeks inputs from experts, industry stakeholders, academia, and the public to create a robust and inclusive policy.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to becoming a leader in responsible AI development.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT, electronics, and internet in India.
AI ethics concerns include bias, privacy, accountability, and job displacement.
India aims to be a global hub for AI, as outlined in 'AI for All' vision.
NITI Aayog has also released a national strategy for AI.
Responsible AI development emphasizes human oversight and societal well-being.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in society.
Analyze the key principles and objectives of India's proposed National AI Ethics Framework and its potential implications for technological innovation.
Evaluate the importance of multi-stakeholder consultations in developing effective AI governance policies.
Examine how India can balance fostering AI innovation with ensuring responsible and equitable use of AI technologies.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized human brain connected to a circuit board, representing human-AI interaction.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

articles.headline: Skill India Mission Releases Mid-Term Progress Report, Highlights Youth Employment Gains
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship today released a mid-term progress report for the Skill India Mission, showcasing significant achievements.
• The report highlights a substantial increase in youth trained and certified under various schemes, leading to improved employability and entrepreneurship.
• Focus areas include industry-aligned courses, digital skilling, and bridging the skill gap in emerging technologies.
• Challenges such as employer perception, quality of training infrastructure, and regional disparities in skill development were also acknowledged.
• The mission aims to further integrate formal education with vocational training to create a future-ready workforce.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Skill India Mission was launched in 2015.
Aims to train over 40 crore people in India in various skills by 2022 (initial target).
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) is the nodal ministry.
PMKVY is the flagship scheme under Skill India Mission.
Focuses on both fresh skilling and reskilling/upskilling of existing workforce.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and major initiatives under the Skill India Mission and its impact on India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the challenges in skilling the vast Indian workforce, including quality assurance, industry linkage, and societal perception.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various skill development programs in addressing unemployment and underemployment among youth.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and technological advancements in enhancing the reach and quality of vocational training.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse young people engaged in vocational training activities.
articles.trivia: India has the largest youth population in the world, with over 600 million people under the age of 25.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Accelerates Infrastructure Projects in Northeast for Strategic Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced a significant acceleration of its infrastructure development projects in India's northeastern states.
• The focus is on enhancing strategic connectivity to border areas, improving troop mobility, and facilitating trade with neighboring countries.
• Projects include the construction of new roads, bridges, and tunnels, especially along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The initiative is crucial for national security and for boosting economic development in the remote regions of the Northeast.
• Advanced engineering techniques and climate-resilient designs are being employed to overcome challenging terrain and weather conditions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Roads, Ports, Airports, Railways etc.
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
prelimsPoints: BRO is a road construction executive force in India that constructs and maintains road networks in border areas.
It operates under the Ministry of Defence.
Northeast India shares borders with China, Myanmar, Bangladesh, Bhutan, and Nepal.
Infrastructure development in border areas is critical for national security and economic integration.
The Sela Tunnel and Atal Tunnel are examples of strategic infrastructure projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust infrastructure development in India's northeastern border regions for national security and regional development.
Analyze the challenges faced by the BRO in executing projects in difficult terrains and adverse weather conditions.
Evaluate the role of enhanced connectivity in facilitating trade, tourism, and people-to-people contact with neighboring countries.
Examine how improved infrastructure can address issues of extremism and underdevelopment in the Northeast.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed road winding through mountainous terrain in the Himalayas, with BRO signage.
articles.trivia: The BRO was formed in 1960 to secure India's borders and develop infrastructure in remote border areas.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Electoral Reforms
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee today tabled its report recommending a series of comprehensive electoral reforms to enhance transparency and fairness in elections.
• Key recommendations include reforms in electoral funding, strengthening the Election Commission's powers, and addressing issues of criminalization of politics.
• The committee suggested a review of the electoral bond scheme, advocating for greater transparency in political donations.
• Proposals for simultaneous elections and a uniform electoral roll were also discussed, with a call for broader political consensus.
• The report is expected to ignite a fresh debate on the urgent need for electoral system overhaul.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Election Commission of India (ECI) is an autonomous constitutional authority (Article 324).
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2018 for political funding.
Criminalization of politics refers to individuals with criminal records contesting elections.
Simultaneous elections propose holding Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections concurrently.
The Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs the conduct of elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges to free and fair elections in India, including electoral funding and criminalization of politics.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Standing Committee on electoral reforms and their potential impact.
Evaluate the arguments for and against proposals like electoral bonds and simultaneous elections.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India and civil society in advocating for and implementing electoral reforms.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a hand casting a vote, symbolizing democratic process and reform.
articles.trivia: India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) and Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) in its elections.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Widening Global Climate Finance Gap for Developing Nations
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) today highlighted a significant and widening gap in global climate finance for developing countries.
• The report indicated that developed nations are falling short of their commitment to provide $100 billion annually for climate action in developing countries.
• This shortfall severely hampers mitigation and adaptation efforts in vulnerable nations, increasing their exposure to climate change impacts.
• Calls were renewed for innovative financing mechanisms, greater transparency, and a clear roadmap for achieving and exceeding climate finance targets.
• The findings are expected to intensify discussions ahead of the next major UN climate summit.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Environmental pollution and degradation
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UNFCCC is an international environmental treaty adopted in 1992.
Developed countries pledged to mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries.
Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing—drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing—that seeks to support mitigation and adaptation actions.
Adaptation refers to adjusting to actual or expected future climate.
Mitigation refers to efforts to reduce or prevent emission of greenhouse gases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of climate finance and its critical importance for developing nations in addressing climate change.
Analyze the reasons behind the persistent shortfall in climate finance commitments by developed countries and its implications.
Evaluate the principles of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of climate finance.
Examine potential innovative financing mechanisms and policy reforms to bridge the global climate finance gap.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a large gap between a target amount and actual funds, with a globe in the background.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) is a legally binding international treaty on climate change, adopted by 196 Parties.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Annual Review Meeting on Indus Waters Treaty
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan today held their annual meeting under the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• Discussions focused on the sharing of hydrological data, flood forecasting, and technical aspects of various run-of-the-river hydropower projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the treaty, despite occasional differences and geopolitical tensions.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, established under the IWT, facilitates these regular interactions to resolve any issues amicably.
• This routine engagement highlights the enduring nature of the treaty, even amidst complex bilateral relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
The treaty allows India to use Western Rivers for limited irrigation, power generation, and domestic use.
Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is a bilateral commission for implementation and dispute resolution.
The treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty in managing water resources and maintaining stability between India and Pakistan.
Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the IWT, highlighting its resilience amidst bilateral tensions.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in bilateral water diplomacy, particularly concerning new hydropower projects.
Examine the role of international mediation (World Bank) in such transboundary water agreements and its implications.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with the allocated rivers for India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty has survived several wars and conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Indian Cities Adopt Green Infrastructure to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities are increasingly adopting green infrastructure solutions to mitigate the severe impacts of the urban heat island (UHI) effect.
• Initiatives include expanding urban green spaces, promoting cool roofs, and using permeable pavements to reduce heat absorption.
• The UHI effect leads to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural regions, exacerbating heat stress and energy consumption.
• These measures aim to improve urban liveability, public health, and energy efficiency, aligning with climate resilience goals.
• Municipal corporations are collaborating with environmental groups and urban planners to integrate these solutions into city master plans.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc.
Changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect occurs when urban areas are significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas.
Caused by dark surfaces (asphalt, concrete) absorbing sunlight, lack of vegetation, and waste heat from human activities.
Green infrastructure includes parks, urban forests, green roofs, and permeable surfaces.
Mitigation strategies help reduce energy consumption for cooling and improve air quality.
The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs promotes sustainable urban development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in Indian cities.
Analyze various green infrastructure solutions being adopted to mitigate UHI and their effectiveness.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale green infrastructure projects in densely populated urban areas.
Examine how UHI mitigation strategies contribute to broader climate adaptation and sustainable urban development goals.
articles.imageDescription: An infographic comparing a hot, concrete city with a cooler city featuring green spaces and light-colored buildings.
articles.trivia: A single mature tree can absorb 48 pounds of carbon dioxide per year and release enough oxygen to support two people.

articles.headline: Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Vikrant Completes Advanced Flight Trials, Nearing Full Operational Capability
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, today successfully completed its advanced flight trials, moving closer to achieving full operational capability.
• The trials involved extensive testing of various aircraft types, including MiG-29K fighters and helicopters, from the carrier's flight deck.
• This milestone significantly boosts India's maritime power projection capabilities and self-reliance in naval defense technology.
• INS Vikrant, commissioned in 2022, is expected to operate with a full complement of aircraft and personnel in the coming months.
• The achievement reinforces India's position as a 'blue-water navy' and a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier.
Commissioned into the Indian Navy in September 2022.
Built by Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL).
It is a STOBAR (Short Take-Off But Arrested Recovery) carrier.
Enhances India's capabilities as a 'blue-water navy' in the Indian Ocean Region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of indigenous aircraft carriers like INS Vikrant for India's naval power and maritime security.
Analyze the challenges and achievements in the indigenization of advanced defense technology, particularly in naval shipbuilding.
Evaluate the role of aircraft carriers in projecting power and safeguarding India's interests in the Indian Ocean Region and beyond.
Examine how INS Vikrant contributes to India's self-reliance in defense and its geopolitical aspirations.
articles.imageDescription: INS Vikrant sailing majestically at sea with fighter jets on its deck.
articles.trivia: The name 'Vikrant' means 'courageous' in Sanskrit, and the first Indian aircraft carrier (R31) was also named Vikrant.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve 10 MMT of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) program is a key component.
3. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for its implementation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The mission aims to achieve 5 MMT (Million Metric Tonnes) of green hydrogen production capacity by 2030. Statement 2 is correct. The SIGHT program provides incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production. Statement 3 is incorrect. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution has been interpreted by the Supreme Court to include the 'Right to Clean Environment' as an integral part of it?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court has interpreted Article 21, which guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty, to encompass the Right to Clean Environment. While Article 48A (DPSP) directs the state to protect and improve the environment, Article 21 provides a fundamental right that can be enforced.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India:
1. It consists of seven members, with four appointed by the Government of India and three from the RBI.
2. Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is the primary measure of inflation targeted by the MPC.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The MPC consists of six members: three from the RBI (including the Governor as ex-officio Chairperson) and three appointed by the Government of India. Statement 2 is correct. This is the explicit mandate of the MPC. Statement 3 is correct. In India, headline inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (Combined) and is the target for the MPC.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to India's space program, what is the primary objective of the Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)?
options: (a) To launch only small satellites into Low Earth Orbit.
(b) To serve as a dedicated vehicle for interplanetary missions to Mars and Venus.
(c) To replace existing PSLV and GSLV rockets as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
(d) To exclusively transport astronauts for India's Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To replace existing PSLV and GSLV rockets as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The NGLV is envisioned as a versatile, heavy-lift launch vehicle that will be cost-effective and reusable. It aims to replace the current workhorse rockets (PSLV and GSLV) to meet the growing demands for various missions, including commercial launches, human spaceflight components, and scientific missions, not just small satellites or interplanetary missions exclusively, nor only Gaganyaan.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a 'Western River' as per the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan?
prelimsMocks.explanation: As per the Indus Waters Treaty, the 'Western Rivers' (whose waters are primarily for Pakistan's unrestricted use, with limited Indian uses) are Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab. The 'Eastern Rivers' (whose waters are primarily for India's unrestricted use) are Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej. Therefore, Ravi is an Eastern River, not a Western River.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy transition and geopolitical positioning. Discuss the key challenges that need to be addressed for its successful implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its strategic importance for energy security, decarbonization, and global leadership. Highlight geopolitical benefits like reducing import dependency. Analyze challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, technology scaling, and policy support. Conclude with a balanced outlook on India's potential.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's proactive stance on environmental issues, particularly regarding air pollution, highlights the judiciary's role in upholding fundamental rights. Discuss the constitutional basis for such interventions and evaluate the effectiveness of judicial activism in environmental governance in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by referencing the Supreme Court's pronouncements on the Right to Clean Environment under Article 21. Explain the constitutional provisions and judicial interpretations (e.g., PILs). Discuss instances of judicial activism in environmental protection. Evaluate its effectiveness in compelling executive action, raising awareness, and filling policy gaps, while also considering potential limitations or overreach.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Act East Policy' has significantly deepened its engagement with ASEAN. Examine the multi-faceted dimensions of India-ASEAN strategic partnership, particularly focusing on maritime security and economic cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region. What are the major challenges in realizing the full potential of this partnership?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the 'Act East Policy' and India-ASEAN strategic partnership. Detail the dimensions of cooperation, emphasizing maritime security (Indo-Pacific vision, joint exercises) and economic ties (trade, investment, supply chains). Discuss shared interests in regional stability. Analyze challenges such as China's growing influence, RCEP withdrawal, non-tariff barriers, and infrastructure gaps. Conclude on the imperative of strengthening this partnership.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Air Quality Action Plans, Citing Right to Life
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today issued strong directives to several state governments and Union Territories regarding the urgent implementation of their Air Quality Action Plans.
• The Court emphasized that clean air is an integral part of the fundamental 'Right to Life' under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• It expressed concern over the persistent high levels of air pollution, especially in major urban centers, despite existing policies and guidelines.
• States have been given a strict deadline to submit compliance reports on measures taken, including curbing stubble burning, industrial emissions, and vehicular pollution.
• The ruling underscores the judiciary's proactive role in environmental governance and public health.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Supreme Court has interpreted Right to Life to include Right to Clean Environment.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) launched in 2019 to reduce PM2.5 and PM10 concentrations by 20-30% by 2024.
Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on environmental policy formulation and implementation in India.
Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in addressing air pollution in India, including socio-economic and technological aspects.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives (e.g., NCAP) and suggest further measures.
Examine the concept of environmental justice and the role of the judiciary in upholding it.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of a polluted city skyline with a clear foreground showing a judge's gavel.
GS2.trivia: Delhi was once ranked the most polluted capital city in the world, often experiencing 'severe' air quality days during winter months.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizes Maritime Security and Economic Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue concluded today, with both sides reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key discussions revolved around enhancing maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, including joint patrols and information sharing.
• Economic integration was a major agenda item, with talks on strengthening supply chain resilience and accelerating trade and investment flows.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its 'Act East' policy, aiming for deeper engagement across political, security, economic, and socio-cultural pillars.
• A joint statement highlighted cooperation on climate change, digital transformation, and people-to-people exchanges.

Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora

India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, upgraded to a Full Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Strategic Partner in 2012.
India's 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy' (1991).
ASEAN comprises 10 member states.
Indo-Pacific region is critical for global trade and security.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the proposed Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, recommending several crucial amendments.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing user consent mechanisms, and refining the definition of 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• Concerns regarding government exemptions and data localization requirements were addressed, with calls for clearer guidelines and independent oversight.
• The committee emphasized the need for a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The bill is expected to be tabled for discussion and passage in the ongoing winter session, potentially with some of the suggested changes incorporated.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
Based on recommendations of the Srikrishna Committee report (2018).
Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 (Puttaswamy judgment).
Aims to establish a Data Protection Board of India.
Distinguishes between data fiduciary (who determines purpose and means of processing) and data principal (individual whose data is processed).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill and its potential impact on individual privacy and the digital economy.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the role of a Data Protection Board in enforcing data protection regulations and addressing grievances.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's bill compares, especially concerning cross-border data flows.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of digital data flowing through a secure network with a padlock icon.
GS2.trivia: The European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is considered one of the strictest data privacy laws globally.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone: Over 50 Crore ABHA IDs Generated
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced today that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has successfully generated over 50 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) IDs.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in creating a digital health ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records and services.
• ABHA IDs allow individuals to link their health records, facilitating easier access for healthcare providers and reducing paperwork.
• The mission aims to standardize digital health infrastructure, promoting interoperability and data security across the healthcare continuum.
• Expanding the network of healthcare facilities and providers registered on ABDM remains a key focus for broader adoption.

National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Part of the broader Ayushman Bharat program, which includes PM-JAY.

GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS2.headline: India and African Nations Discuss Feasibility of New Trade Corridor for Enhanced Connectivity
GS2.explainer:
• India and several African nations initiated discussions today on the feasibility of establishing a dedicated trade corridor to boost economic ties.
• The proposed corridor aims to streamline logistics, reduce trade costs, and facilitate greater movement of goods, services, and investments between India and Africa.
• Discussions focused on infrastructure development, digital connectivity, and harmonizing trade regulations to create a seamless economic pathway.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to strengthening South-South cooperation and leveraging Africa's growth potential.
• Experts highlight the potential for collaboration in areas like renewable energy, agriculture, and healthcare through this enhanced connectivity.

GS2.headline: Parliament Debates Centre-State Fiscal Devolution Amidst GST Compensation Cess Discussions
GS2.explainer:
• The winter session of Parliament witnessed a heated debate today on the issues of Centre-State fiscal devolution and the future of GST compensation cess.
• Several states raised concerns over reduced fiscal space, demanding a clear roadmap for revenue sharing beyond the GST compensation period.
• The Union Finance Minister highlighted the recommendations of the latest Finance Commission and measures taken to support states' finances.
• Opposition parties called for greater transparency and autonomy for states in managing their fiscal resources.
• The discussions underscore the ongoing complexities of fiscal federalism in India and the need for cooperative solutions.

GS2.headline: Skill India Mission Releases Mid-Term Progress Report, Highlights Youth Employment Gains
GS2.explainer:

• The report highlights a substantial increase in youth trained and certified under various schemes, leading to improved employability and entrepreneurship.
• Focus areas include industry-aligned courses, digital skilling, and bridging the skill gap in emerging technologies.
• Challenges such as employer perception, quality of training infrastructure, and regional disparities in skill development were also acknowledged.
• The mission aims to further integrate formal education with vocational training to create a future-ready workforce.

GS2.explainer:

• Key recommendations include reforms in electoral funding, strengthening the Election Commission's powers, and addressing issues of criminalization of politics.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Detailed Implementation Strategy for National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:

• The strategy focuses on phased manufacturing capabilities, demand creation mechanisms, and infrastructure development for green hydrogen production and utilization.
• Key components include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing, pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors, and a robust R&D framework.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing significantly to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are highlighted as crucial for achieving the mission's ambitious targets.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, reflecting a cautious approach to inflation management while supporting economic growth.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the MPC highlighted global uncertainties and potential risks from food price volatility.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target, indicating that a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance would continue.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts would depend on sustained disinflation and robust economic data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
MPC sets the policy interest rates to achieve inflation target (currently 4% +/- 2%).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Headline inflation is measured by Consumer Price Index (CPI) in India.
RBI's primary objective is price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate.
Discuss the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy (e.g., banking, industry, consumers).
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth objectives.
Examine the role of global economic factors in shaping India's monetary policy.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the RBI logo with upward-trending inflation and downward-trending growth lines.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV)
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial hot-fire test for the semi-cryogenic engine of its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• This test marks a significant milestone in the development of NGLV, which is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
• NGLV aims to replace the existing PSLV and GSLV rockets, catering to the growing demands of India's space missions, including human spaceflight and commercial launches.
• The vehicle will utilize green propellants and modular architecture, enhancing its versatility and environmental sustainability.
• ISRO plans for NGLV to be operational by the early 2030s, positioning India strongly in the global space launch market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: NGLV aims to replace PSLV and GSLV.
It is designed as a reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle.
Utilizes semi-cryogenic engines and green propellants.
Expected to be operational by early 2030s.
ISRO is India's national space agency, headquartered in Bengaluru.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing Next-Generation Launch Vehicles for India's space program and national interests.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements involved in designing and testing semi-cryogenic engines and reusable launch vehicles.
Evaluate the potential impact of NGLV on India's commercial space capabilities and its role in the global space market.
Examine how NGLV aligns with India's long-term goals in space exploration and human spaceflight (e.g., Gaganyaan).
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the NGLV taking off, with the ISRO logo prominently displayed.
GS3.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Projected at 7.2%, Driven by Services and Manufacturing Sector
GS3.explainer:
• Leading economic think tanks and rating agencies today released their projections for India's Q3 (October-December) GDP growth, averaging around 7.2%.
• The robust growth is attributed to strong performance in the services sector, particularly financial and IT services, and a resilient manufacturing output.
• Festival season demand and increased government capital expenditure are cited as key drivers contributing to domestic consumption and investment.
• While global headwinds persist, India's domestic demand-led recovery continues to show resilience, positioning it as a fast-growing major economy.
• However, concerns remain regarding rural demand recovery and potential impacts of global commodity price fluctuations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific period.
India's financial year runs from April 1 to March 31.
Q3 refers to the October-December quarter.
Key drivers of GDP include private consumption, government consumption, investment, and net exports.
National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI releases official GDP data.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's projected GDP growth in Q3 and the role of different economic sectors.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining high economic growth in India amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government fiscal and monetary policies in fostering economic recovery and stability.
Examine the implications of India's growth trajectory for employment generation and poverty reduction.
GS3.imageDescription: A bar graph showing India's GDP growth trend with various sectoral contributions.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2030.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Urban Affairs Launches New Framework for Urban Flood Resilience Planning
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today unveiled a comprehensive framework to enhance urban flood resilience across Indian cities.
• The framework emphasizes integrated water management, nature-based solutions, and upgrading existing drainage infrastructure.
• It mandates the preparation of city-specific Urban Flood Resilience Plans, incorporating early warning systems and community participation.
• The initiative comes in response to increasing frequency and intensity of urban flooding events exacerbated by climate change and unplanned urbanization.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to states and local bodies for effective implementation.

Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is often caused by inadequate drainage, encroachment on natural water bodies, and intense rainfall.

Smart Cities Mission aims to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
Nature-based solutions include green infrastructure like rain gardens and permeable pavements.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs is the nodal ministry for urban development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the primary causes of urban flooding in India and its socio-economic consequences.
Analyze the key features of the new Urban Flood Resilience Planning framework and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of urban planning, infrastructure development, and community engagement in building flood-resilient cities.
Examine how climate change exacerbates urban flooding and the importance of adaptation strategies.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city with a green infrastructure overlay, showing water flowing into designated green spaces.
GS3.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a major natural drain, has been significantly narrowed and polluted, contributing to severe flooding.

GS3.headline: CERT-In Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) today released updated guidelines for cybersecurity practices targeting critical information infrastructure (CII).
• The revised framework mandates stricter compliance for organizations operating in sectors like energy, finance, telecommunications, and transport.
• Key directives include implementing multi-factor authentication, regular security audits, and establishing robust incident response protocols.
• The move aims to bolster India's defenses against sophisticated cyber threats and protect essential services from potential disruptions.
• Collaboration between government agencies and private sector entities is stressed for effective implementation and threat intelligence sharing.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention

Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds layers of security beyond just a password.

Examine the importance of public-private partnerships and international cooperation in combating global cyber threats.
GS3.imageDescription: A network of digital lines forming a shield, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: The Stuxnet computer worm, discovered in 2010, was one of the first known cyberweapons used to target industrial control systems.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches Enhanced National Plan for Wetlands Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched an upgraded National Plan for Wetlands Conservation and Management, focusing on integrated approaches.
• The plan emphasizes scientific inventory, ecological restoration, community participation, and sustainable utilization of wetland resources.
• It aims to protect and restore India's diverse wetlands, which play crucial roles in biodiversity, water purification, and climate change mitigation.
• Special attention will be given to Ramsar Sites and other ecologically sensitive wetlands, with enhanced monitoring and enforcement mechanisms.
• The initiative seeks to align national efforts with international conventions like the Ramsar Convention.

Biodiversity

Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal agency for wetland conservation.
Wetland (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017, provide the regulatory framework.

GS3.trivia: The world's largest wetland is the Pantanal, primarily located in Brazil, Bolivia, and Paraguay.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates Public Consultation on National AI Ethics Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a public consultation process for India's proposed National AI Ethics Framework.
• The framework aims to guide the ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across various sectors, ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability.
• Key principles include human-centric AI, data privacy, bias mitigation, and responsible innovation.
• The consultation seeks inputs from experts, industry stakeholders, academia, and the public to create a robust and inclusive policy.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to becoming a leader in responsible AI development.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Computers

AI ethics concerns include bias, privacy, accountability, and job displacement.

GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Accelerates Infrastructure Projects in Northeast for Strategic Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced a significant acceleration of its infrastructure development projects in India's northeastern states.
• The focus is on enhancing strategic connectivity to border areas, improving troop mobility, and facilitating trade with neighboring countries.
• Projects include the construction of new roads, bridges, and tunnels, especially along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• The initiative is crucial for national security and for boosting economic development in the remote regions of the Northeast.
• Advanced engineering techniques and climate-resilient designs are being employed to overcome challenging terrain and weather conditions.

Infrastructure development in border areas is critical for national security and economic integration.
The Sela Tunnel and Atal Tunnel are examples of strategic infrastructure projects.

GS3.trivia: The BRO was formed in 1960 to secure India's borders and develop infrastructure in remote border areas.

GS3.headline: UN Report Highlights Widening Global Climate Finance Gap for Developing Nations
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) today highlighted a significant and widening gap in global climate finance for developing countries.
• The report indicated that developed nations are falling short of their commitment to provide $100 billion annually for climate action in developing countries.
• This shortfall severely hampers mitigation and adaptation efforts in vulnerable nations, increasing their exposure to climate change impacts.
• Calls were renewed for innovative financing mechanisms, greater transparency, and a clear roadmap for achieving and exceeding climate finance targets.
• The findings are expected to intensify discussions ahead of the next major UN climate summit.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Environmental pollution and degradation
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UNFCCC is an international environmental treaty adopted in 1992.
Developed countries pledged to mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries.
Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing—drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing—that seeks to support mitigation and adaptation actions.
Adaptation refers to adjusting to actual or expected future climate.
Mitigation refers to efforts to reduce or prevent emission of greenhouse gases.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of climate finance and its critical importance for developing nations in addressing climate change.

Examine potential innovative financing mechanisms and policy reforms to bridge the global climate finance gap.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing a large gap between a target amount and actual funds, with a globe in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) is a legally binding international treaty on climate change, adopted by 196 Parties.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Vikrant Completes Advanced Flight Trials, Nearing Full Operational Capability
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, today successfully completed its advanced flight trials, moving closer to achieving full operational capability.
• The trials involved extensive testing of various aircraft types, including MiG-29K fighters and helicopters, from the carrier's flight deck.
• This milestone significantly boosts India's maritime power projection capabilities and self-reliance in naval defense technology.
• INS Vikrant, commissioned in 2022, is expected to operate with a full complement of aircraft and personnel in the coming months.
• The achievement reinforces India's position as a 'blue-water navy' and a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.

Examine how INS Vikrant contributes to India's self-reliance in defense and its geopolitical aspirations.

GS3.trivia: The name 'Vikrant' means 'courageous' in Sanskrit, and the first Indian aircraft carrier (R31) was also named Vikrant.
